description: Frozen pre-registration for SwitchSync Markets P1 synthetic experiments. NO real market data is used anywhere in this contract.

fhn.note_sigma_inter: sigma_12=1.5 is used for the double-layer reproduction. This DIFFERS from the paper's 0.1 (used at N=400). Reason: inter-layer coupling acts only on the activator and the transverse spectrum is N-dependent; at our tractable N the sync threshold for 25%-density switching sits near sigma_12~1.0-1.5. The reproduction target is QUALITATIVE (Gate G0), not the absolute threshold. Documented in engel_paper_audit.md and financial_translation_audit.md.

reproduction.acceptance_G0: For sigma_inter=1.5: fast switching (T_swt<=10) synchronizes (tail E12<0.02) in a majority of seeds AND slow switching (T_swt>=160) does not; maximum T_swt that synchronizes is finite and decreases relative to fastest.
reproduction.failure_G0: Fast switching fails to synchronize, OR slow synchronizes as readily as fast.

msf_minimal.note: Reproduces the sign structure of the minimal-system transverse Lyapunov exponent (paper Psi). Coarse grid.

surrogate_causal.acceptance_G1: fast contraction rate gamma exceeds static_sparse and slow by a margin > 3 pooled std across seeds.
surrogate_causal.acceptance_G2: shuffled_order at INTERMEDIATE dwell changes gamma by more than the seed-noise band relative to ordered intermediate; if not, record that evidence favors aggregate connectivity over temporal order.
surrogate_causal.note_average_graph: average_graph = static with per-pair weight = occupancy (=density_ratio). Per the brief, switching must BEAT the average graph to count as an isolated mechanism. Fast-switching/averaging theory predicts fast switching APPROACHES the average graph; we report the sign of (gamma_fast - gamma_average) honestly.

surrogate_stages.acceptance_G3: fast-switching contraction advantage over static_sparse persists (gamma_fast - gamma_static > 0) through mild heterogeneity; record the stage at which it disappears.

identifiability.acceptance_G4: estimated active-link precision AND recall > 0.6 out-of-sample (causal, no future data), and estimated contraction correlates with true contraction (Pearson > 0.5); factor-induced false links stay below precision floor.
identifiability.failure_G4: precision or recall <= 0.5, or common-factor removal is required to avoid pervasive false links AND still fails.
